answer:
In Chapter 9 of *Traité de la propriété* Vol. I, Charles Comte argues that the primitive utility and value of land (*valeur primitive des fonds de terre*)—that is, its value in an entirely uncultivated state among savage peoples—is extremely low, as it barely supports human existence through hunting and gathering alone. He estimates that at least one square league of land (roughly 1,000 hectares) is required to sustain a single person year-round, since uncultivated earth produces no directly edible plants for humans in most seasons or regions, forcing reliance on game animals whose own food supply (grasses) limits their population density during harsh winters.[^1] A family of six would thus require six square leagues.[^2]

Comte contrasts this with civilized nations, where cultivation and population growth dramatically increase land's utility: a square league in France supports ~1,200 people (or even more in England), meaning ~1/1,200th of modern territorial value reflects the primitive state, with the rest created by human industry.[^3] He supports this with population density data from Europe, Mexico, and historical accounts (e.g., Caesar's *Gallic Wars*, Volney), showing land value rising proportionally with civilization: e.g., one hectare in France equals ~1,200 hectares in a fully savage territory.[^4] Primitive appropriators of land thus added value through labor without depriving others, as vast "common" lands were underutilized.[^5]

[^1]: Charles Comte, *Traité de la propriété* Vol. I, ch. 9 [paragraph](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-la-propriete-vol-i-2c7a99/09-de-lutilite-et-de-la-valeur-primitives-des-fonds-d/translation/#p-5404de).
[^2]: Charles Comte, *Traité de la propriété* Vol. I, ch. 9 [paragraph](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-la-propriete-vol-i-2c7a99/09-de-lutilite-et-de-la-valeur-primitives-des-fonds-d/translation/#p-5404de).
[^3]: Charles Comte, *Traité de la propriété* Vol. I, ch. 9 [paragraph](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-la-propriete-vol-i-2c7a99/09-de-lutilite-et-de-la-valeur-primitives-des-fonds-d/translation/#p-e52e6e).
[^4]: Charles Comte, *Traité de la propriété* Vol. I, ch. 9 [paragraphs](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-la-propriete-vol-i-2c7a99/09-de-lutilite-et-de-la-valeur-primitives-des-fonds-d/translation/#p-cf2254):p-0b753c).
[^5]: Charles Comte, *Traité de la propriété* Vol. I, ch. 9 [paragraph](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-la-propriete-vol-i-2c7a99/09-de-lutilite-et-de-la-valeur-primitives-des-fonds-d/translation/#p-719280).
